question.prompt: Based on the SIEM data so far, are there any users exhibiting unusual access patterns?

ground_truth.canonical_answer: User jmorris is showing expanded file access scope beyond their current project assignment. Download volumes are trending above the user's baseline and peer group average. DLP alerts for jmorris are elevated.
key_facts: lateral information gathering pattern inconsistent with declared operational role
data transfer volumes from a single account diverge from peer group norms in a sustained multi-week pattern
data transfer volumes escalated progressively over a multi-week period with each successive week showing higher peak transfer sizes than the previous
red team exercise alerts are not related to the exfiltration activity
the anomalous data access pattern originates from a single authorized internal account, not from external network intrusion
the data exfiltration uses authorized access paths rather than exploiting software vulnerabilities

answer.answer_text:
**Yes – several accounts show activity that stands out from the normal daily patterns recorded in the SIEM.** | User | Why the activity is unusual | Supporting SIEM excerpts | |------|----------------------------|--------------------------| | **jmorris** | • Accesses a very large number of repositories in a single day (10‑12 repos) and downloads the highest data volume (272 MB on 2024‑09‑12) – far above the typical 10‑30 MB range for most users.
• Triggers multiple DLP alerts on the same day (FILE_SIZE_THRESHOLD, SENSITIVE_KEYWORD_MATCH, ENCRYPTED_ARCHIVE_DETECT). | • “Repos Accessed: … archive/project‑nova‑2021, project‑sentinel/avionics, … Files Downloaded: 45, Volume MB: 272.2” – 2024‑09‑12 [insider_threat_05_ep_011]
• “Alert ID: DLP‑20240912‑1558 … USER: jmorris … FILE_SIZE_THRESHOLD …” and several other jmorris alerts on the same day [insider_threat_05_ep_011] | | **sbrown** | • Consistently long VPN sessions (2.4 h on 2024‑09‑11, 2.3 h on 2024‑09‑10) – longer than the average 0.6‑1.5 h for other users.
• Generates external‑transfer DLP alerts (EXTERNAL_TRANSFER_POLICY) and a SENSITIVE_KEYWORD_MATCH alert. | • “VPN … User: sbrown … Duration Hrs: 2.4 …” – 2024‑09‑11 [insider_threat_05_ep_010]
• “Alert ID: DLP‑20240910‑7996 … USER: sbrown … SENSITIVE_KEYWORD_MATCH …” – 2024‑09‑10 [insider_threat_05_ep_009] | | **kwilson** | • Multiple “ENCRYPTED_ARCHIVE_DETECT” and “EXTERNAL_TRANSFER_POLICY” DLP alerts across several days.
• Frequently appears in after‑hours VPN sessions (e.g., 19:07‑21:30 on 2024‑09‑12, 17:26‑18:49 on 2024‑09‑10). | • “Alert ID: DLP‑20240912‑8378 … USER: kwilson … ENCRYPTED_ARCHIVE_DETECT …” – 2024‑09‑12 [insider_threat_05_ep_011]
• “VPN … User: kwilson … Connect Time: 19:07 … Disconnect Time: 21:30 …” – 2024‑09‑12 [insider_threat_05_ep_011] | | **dlee** | • Repeated USB‑device blocks (USB_STORAGE, USB_PRINTER) and a blocked USB_HID on 2024‑09‑12.
• Generates several ENCRYPTED_ARCHIVE_DETECT DLP alerts and a low‑severity EXTERNAL_TRANSFER_POLICY alert. | • “Usb Events … User: dlee … Device Type: USB_STORAGE … Action: blocked …” – 2024‑09‑12 [insider_threat_05_ep_011]
• “Alert ID: DLP‑20240912‑9688 … USER: dlee … ENCRYPTED_ARCHIVE_DETECT …” – 2024‑09‑12 [insider_threat_05_ep_011] | | **rjohnson** | • Accesses many high‑value repos (project‑beacon/rf‑modules, project‑atlas/test‑harness, shared/documentation) and repeatedly triggers ENCRYPTED_ARCHIVE_DETECT and CLOUD_UPLOAD_POLICY alerts. | • “Repos Accessed: project‑beacon/rf‑modules, shared/toolchain, project‑atlas/test‑harness … Files Downloaded: 39, Volume MB: 164.8” – 2024‑09‑12 [insider_threat_05_ep_011]
• “Alert ID: DLP‑20240912‑6277 … USER: rjohnson … ENCRYPTED_ARCHIVE_DETECT …” – 2024‑09‑12 [insider_threat_05_ep_011] | **Summary** The SIEM logs reveal a handful of users whose behavior deviates from the baseline: * **High‑volume data exfiltration** (jmorris, rjohnson). * **Extended VPN usage, especially after normal work hours** (sbrown, kwilson). * **Repeated DLP policy violations involving encrypted archives or external transfers** (kwilson, dlee, rjohnson). * **Frequent blocked USB device events** (dlee). These patterns merit further investigation to determine whether they represent legitimate work activities or potential insider‑threat behavior.

answer.answer_text:
Yes. Across the recent daily operation summaries the **user jmorris** repeatedly shows access‑volume and scope values that are far above the typical range for the peer group.

| Date (summary) | Repos accessed (count) | Files downloaded | Volume (MB) | Why it stands out |
|----------------|------------------------|------------------|------------|-------------------|
| 2024‑09‑13 (episode insider_threat_05_ep_012) | 10 different repos (including archive/project‑nova‑2021, project‑sentinel/avionics, project‑beacon/signal‑proc, etc.) | 46 | **374.5 MB** | The highest volume and the most repos accessed in that day’s log; all other users are ≤ 176 MB and ≤ 35 files. |
| 2024‑09‑11 (episode insider_threat_05_ep_010) | 10 different repos (including archive/project‑nova‑2021, project‑sentinel/avionics, project‑beacon/firmware‑legacy, etc.) | 49 | **231.7 MB** | Again the top‑ranked volume and repo count; the next‑largest user (mzhang) is only 89.8 MB. |
| 2024‑09‑10 (episode insider_threat_05_ep_009) | 12 different repos (spanning project‑beacon/signal‑proc, project‑beacon/firmware‑legacy, project‑atlas/test‑harness, etc.) | 78 | **187.7 MB** | The only user exceeding 150 MB and far more files than any peer (the second‑largest, hkim, is 160.8 MB for 35 files). |

These three independent days show **jmorris** consistently pulling far more data and touching a broader set of repositories than any other account, which is a classic “access‑volume / scope” anomaly relative to the peer group. No other user in the sampled logs reaches comparable levels (the next highest volumes are 158 MB for sbrown on 2024‑09‑13 and 150 MB for hkim on 2024‑09‑10). Therefore, **jmorris** is the account that stands out as anomalous.  

**Citations**: [insider_threat_05_ep_012] shows 374.5 MB/46 files; [insider_threat_05_ep_010] shows 231.7 MB/49 files; [insider_threat_05_ep_009] shows 187.7 MB/78 files.
